question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setDigestAlgorithm()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that digestAlgorithm is stored as an ordinal integer (@Enumerated without EnumType.STRING), causing a database value of 0 to map to SHA_384, and if a new DigestAlgorithm enum value is inserted before SHA_384 in a future version, causing all existing records to map to the wrong algorithm, leading to hash verification failures?
